body_txt: A character says or does something intended to elicit an enthusiastic response from a group watching him, such as the audience in a theatre or a band of comrades. But instead of the reaction he expects, he hears nothing but the sound of crickets chirping. The idea is that the crowd has gone so silent that very faint, ambient sounds that are usually inaudible are now the only sounds to hear. Crickets themselves are now regarded as something of a cliche, but the trope is still common with a variety of other sounds that call attention to the silence, including blowing wind, a wolf howling in the distance, microphone feedback, a baby crying, or an awkward cough from the crowd. Alternatively, the reaction might be visual such as a tumbling tumbleweed or a bird flying by , which is popular in anime. This trope often occurs when an audience reacts with Stunned Silence , refuses to participate , or has all left without the speaker's notice . A more actively resentful reaction will often include Produce Pelting or a sarcastic Rimshot .
